findings.evidence: The file already exposes the general API at `Diagram.lean:254` (`mem_pointSet_relabelColumns`) and defines `swapColumns` as the specialization `relabelColumns (Equiv.swap a b)` at `Diagram.lean:264`; the new theorem at `Diagram.lean:308` duplicates that special case instead of proving the natural relabeling form.
findings.fix: Add a general theorem such as `p ∈ x.pointSet ∩ (x.relabelColumns κ).pointSet ↔ p ∈ x.pointSet ∧ κ p.1 = p.1`, then derive the swap-column version using `κ = Equiv.swap a b` and `a ≠ b`.
findings.issue: `mem_pointSet_inter_swapColumns_iff` is under-generalized: the same argument characterizes intersection with any column relabeling by the fixed columns of the permutation.

summary: One new `GridState` bookkeeping lemma is stated only for column swaps even though the surrounding API already has the natural `relabelColumns` abstraction. Prove the permutation-level intersection theorem first and derive the swap specialization from it.
